---
title: Greenwood
toc: false
---

**Modern survival analysis for Python.**

Greenwood is a time-to-event modeling library built for 2026: it works on pandas, Polars,
PyArrow, and anything else [narwhals](https://narwhals-dev.github.io/narwhals/) supports;
it is validated to tolerance against R's `survival` package; it visualizes with
[plotnine](https://plotnine.org/); and it plugs into the Great Tables ecosystem for
publication-quality tables. The name is a nod to Greenwood's formula, the classic variance
estimator for the Kaplan-Meier curve.

## Status

This release provides the `Surv` response object (right, left, interval, and
counting-process censoring, with left truncation and multi-state endpoints), the
narwhals-native risk-set kernel, the non-parametric estimators `KaplanMeier` (survival
function, Greenwood confidence intervals, median and quantiles, restricted mean survival
time) and `NelsonAalen` (cumulative hazard), the `logrank_test` (log-rank and the
G-rho / Fleming-Harrington family), `CoxPH` proportional hazards regression, and plotnine
visualization with aligned risk tables. Every statistic is validated to tolerance against
R. Parametric models, competing risks, and Cox extensions arrive in later releases; see the
[roadmap](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/ROADMAP.md).

## A first look

```python
import greenwood as gw

# Load a bundled dataset and build a response (lung codes 1 = censored, 2 = dead).
df = gw.load_dataset("lung")
y = gw.Surv.right(df["time"], event=(df["status"] == 2))

# Kaplan-Meier survival, stratified, with log-log confidence intervals.
km = gw.KaplanMeier(conf_type="log-log").fit(y, by=df["sex"])
km.to_dataframe().head()
km.median(ci=True)

# Compare groups with the log-rank test.
gw.logrank_test(y, group=df["sex"])

# Publication-quality curves with an aligned numbers-at-risk table.
gw.plot_survival(km, risk_table=True)

# Cox proportional hazards, with broom-style hazard ratios.
cox = gw.CoxPH().fit(y, df[["age", "sex"]])
gw.tidy.tidy(cox, exponentiate=True)
```
